label: Uncomplicated folliculitis without abscess or cellulitis
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated folliculitis.
No abscess, boil, carbuncle, cellulitis, systemic illness, high-risk host pathway, genital pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Abscess, boil, carbuncle, cellulitis, rapidly spreading infection, or systemic illness.
Genital involvement, immunocompromised host, diabetes/PVD/neuropathy risk, recurrent disease, hot tub outbreak, or public-health-directed plan.
Medication selection not supplied by clinician.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, or dermatology if symptoms are not improving or if this keeps coming back.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports folliculitis framing, hair-follicle rash or pustules, shaving/friction triggers, and escalation for spreading, recurrent, or higher-risk infection. Autonomous Phase 1601-1650 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated folliculitis without abscess, boil, carbuncle, cellulitis, systemic illness, high-risk host pathway, genital pathway, recurrent/outbreak context, or specialist-directed plan.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. MedlinePlus supports folliculitis framing, hair-follicle rash or pustules, shaving/friction triggers, and escalation for spreading, recurrent, or higher-risk infection. Autonomous Phase 1601-1650 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated folliculitis without abscess, boil, carbuncle, cellulitis, systemic illness, high-risk host pathway, genital pathway, recurrent/outbreak context, or specialist-directed plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated folliculitis
no abscess, boil, carbuncle, cellulitis, or systemic illness documented
no high-risk host, genital, recurrent, outbreak, or specialist-directed pathway
